articles.title: From Dissonance to Compassion: Does a Loving-Kindness Meditation Amplify Joy Mimicry and Attenuate Reactive Frowns to Disgust?
articles.authors: Heidi Mauersberger, Till Kastendieck, Daniel Huppertz, Patrysya Lacorte, Patrick Kotzur, Ursula Hess
articles.abstract: Emotional mimicry—the automatic matching of another person's emotional display—fosters social bonding, but only when an affiliative emotion aligns with its context. We tested whether a brief Loving-Kindness Meditation (LKM) could broaden affiliative responsiveness to both congruent and incongruent emotional displays. In an online study ( N = 214), participants watched targets expressing joy or disgust in scenes that were either contextually congruent (e.g., smiling at a funny clip, showing disgust at a disgusting clip) or incongruent (e.g., smiling at a disgusting clip, showing disgust at a funny clip). Facial muscle activity was assessed using OpenFace and subjective ratings of closeness and appropriateness were obtained. We found mimicry combined with high closeness ratings only for joy in a matching context, whereas incongruent and antagonistic displays evoked disapproval rather than affiliation. Critically, LKM increased mimicry to congruent joy and reduced frowning responses to incongruent disgust, without altering appropriateness judgments or perceived closeness. These findings demonstrate that even a brief LKM can selectively boost mimicry of context-appropriate affiliative emotions and temper reactive disapproval when negativity violates social norms, highlighting LKM's potential to foster empathy in emotionally discordant situations.
articles.keywords: Disgust; Psychology; Mimicry; Closeness; Imitation; Empathy; Cognitive psychology; Social psychology; Prosocial behavior; Facial expression; Perception
articles.category: Emotion, Facial Expression, Face, Social Cognition
articles.summary: 本研究通过在线实验考察了慈悲冥想对情绪模仿的影响。结果显示，短暂的慈悲冥想能增强对情境一致快乐的模仿反应，并减少对不一致厌恶的皱眉反应，而不改变对情绪适当性或亲密感的判断。这表明慈悲冥想在情绪不协调情境中具有选择性促进亲社会反应的潜力。

articles.title: Power in fantasy stories and life story episodes: an exploratory study on meaning in life in elderlies from Cameroon, the Czech Republic, Germany, and Hong Kong
articles.authors: Jan Hofer, Alma Au, Iva Poláčková Šolcová, Peter Tavel, Teresa Tsien
articles.abstract: Living in accordance with the true self, that is reflected by significant components of personality (e.g., basic motives), relates to psychological well-being and mental health. While findings for the motives of affiliation/relatedness and achievement/competence are relatively straightforward, theoretical approaches make different assumptions on consequences of striving for power, defined as a concern for having impact on others' behaviors and emotions (Winter, 1973). Self-determination theory argues that pursuing power-related strivings deflects from satisfying basic psychological needs. In contrast, the power motive represents an integral part of the motivational system in (implicit) motive disposition theory and its satisfaction predicts well-being. In the present study, we conducted an exploratory analysis to examine whether a high need for power is associated with power-motivational content in life story episodes. Moreover, we examined whether participants report higher levels of meaning in life when power-motivational content in life story episodes matches the strength of their implicit power motive. To scrutinize the generalizability of assumed relationships, data were assessed among older adults ( n = 450) in Cameroon, China (Hong Kong), Czech Republic, and Germany. In sum, analyses provided weak support for the association between nPower and power-motivational content in life story episodes. However, our findings suggest that, regardless of cultural background, power-motivated individuals report higher levels of meaning in life the more their recalled life episodes reflect power-related themes. Yet, it is crucial to confirm our initial findings in future hypothesis-testing studies, particularly with regard to factors that enable individuals to have motive-relevant experiences in life and to recall them. Effects of implicit motives on the relationship between life review and psychological well-being are discussed.
articles.keywords: Psychology; Meaning; Generalizability theory; Exploratory research; Power; Social psychology; Personality; Fantasy; Content; Life satisfaction
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了来自喀麦隆、捷克、德国和香港的老年人的权力动机与生活故事中权力主题的匹配程度，及其对人生意义的影响。结果发现，权力动机与生活故事中的权力内容关联较弱，但无论文化背景如何，权力动机较强的个体在回忆更多权力相关生活事件时报告更高的人生意义。

articles.title: Beauty is in the brain of the beholder: Psychological and physiological effects of expertise on the perception of classicist and modernist buildings.
articles.authors: Anita D'Anselmo, Lorenzo Pellegrini, Giulia Prete, Panagiotis Mavros, Gianluca Malatesta, Caterina Palestini, Alberto Di Domenico, Nicola Mammarella, Luca Tommasi, Laura Bonanni
articles.journal: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts
articles.publish_info: Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts, 2026-06-01
articles.abstract: Although aesthetic appreciation is a subjective experience, classicist architecture is often judged as pleasant by many people, whereas modernist buildings may be more appreciated by experts, being somehow disliked by naive observers. In the present study, we aimed at investigating psychological (pleasantness evaluation) and physiological (electroencephalography) differences between architects (expert group) and nonarchitects (naive group) for classicist versus modernist buildings, shown as entire photos (far perspective) and as details of the same buildings (near perspective). At a psychological level, we confirmed that modernist buildings are more appreciated by experts and classicist buildings by naive. Electroencephalography revealed higher amplitude in the posterior P1 event-related potential component for classicist compared with modernist buildings only in experts. The subsequent P2 component was faster in latency for classicist than modernist buildings in naive, but it was faster for modernist than classicist buildings in experts. Perspective also affected P2, with a faster peak in the left hemisphere for modernist buildings when stimuli were shown from far with respect to near in both subgroups, and the same result was observed for the classicist style but only in the expert group. Finally, at frontal sites, a faster N400 was recorded for modernist compared with classicist architecture, only in expert participants. These results confirm the difference in aesthetic appreciation between experts and nonexperts, suggesting differences in the cortical responses associated with style and perspective.
articles.keywords: Beauty; Perception; Psychology; Aesthetics; Cognitive psychology; Cognitive science; Creativity; Social psychology; Aesthetic experience
articles.summary: 本研究考察了专业知识（建筑师与非建筑师）对古典主义和现代主义建筑美学感知的影响。通过愉悦度评价和脑电图测量，发现专家更欣赏现代主义建筑，而非专家更欣赏古典主义建筑，且两组在神经反应（如P1、P2、N400成分）上存在差异，表明风格和视角对美学体验具有调节作用。

articles.title: About time? The role of time perspective in the priority for positive over negative emotion in attention.
articles.abstract: Compared to younger adults, older adults often prioritise processing positive information and deprioritize processing negative information. Known as the positivity effect, its mechanisms remain debated. One explanation of the positivity effect is that older adults are influenced by a sense of limited time left in life to focus more on emotional well-being compared with younger adults. Previous research shows that experimentally manipulating time perspective can mimic the positivity effect in younger adults, however, such findings have relied on designs where participants can reflect on emotional images they are viewing. In the current study, we examined if time perspective manipulations would similarly elicit positivity effect patterns in younger adults using an emotion-attention task with a rapid stimuli display. Across three experiments (N = 236, N = 431, and N = 259), we found that time perspective had little influence on attentional biases toward positive over negative emotional stimuli in younger adults. Additionally, when we incorporated a memory task into the design, effects of time perspective were not consistent with previous findings - in fact, patterns were in the opposite direction. We discuss these results as they relate to theories and the advantages and disadvantages of tasks used to examine the positivity effect.
articles.summary: 本研究通过三个实验探讨了时间视角对年轻成人注意力中积极情绪优先于消极情绪的影响。结果显示，时间视角操纵对注意力偏差的影响有限，且在整合记忆任务后，效应方向与先前关于积极效应的研究不一致。这表明时间视角可能不是积极效应的主要机制，或任务设计差异导致了不同发现。

articles.title: Remembering affect between moments: assessing peak-end effects in continuous affect measures.
articles.authors: Leonie Cloos, Wolf Vanpaemel, Eva Ceulemans, Peter Kuppens
articles.abstract: In experience sampling (ESM) studies, participants are measured repeatedly to track momentary affect changes. The data is collected in specific time intervals, leaving gaps between assessments. To capture affective changes between assessments, we developed a measure where participants retrospectively draw their affect in a continuous drawing. This approach, while more complete, may be influenced by retrospective biases, such as the peak-end effect, where intense or recent moments are remembered disproportionately. In an ESM study, 115 participants rated their momentary positive and negative affect six times daily, drew affect changes between assessments, reported events, and completed one daily measurement burst with up to six momentary ratings. We examined the divergence of the retrospective continuous affect drawings from the momentary affect and event ratings, respectively. For positive affect, intensity and recency were associated with lower divergence; the association was nonlinear. Divergence was greatest at low intensity and reduced at moderate to high levels. For negative affect, intensity was associated with higher divergence and recency with lower divergence. The effect of intensity diminished over time, indicating that divergence is mostly determined by recency. Findings were similar for events, except that for negative events, there was no recency effect. Future research should consider the effect of time and intensity, especially the differential effect of positive and negative intensity, on short retrospective ESM measures.
articles.keywords: Experience sampling; affect dynamics; continuous affect; peak-end rule; retrospective self-report
articles.summary: 本研究评估了经验取样方法中回顾性连续情感绘图的峰终效应偏差。对于正性情感，强度和近因性降低偏差但呈非线性关系；对于负性情感，强度增加偏差而近因性降低偏差，且强度效应随时间减弱。这些发现提示在情感动态研究中需考虑回顾性测量的偏差。

articles.title: Others' facial and bodily expressions of emotion modulate willingness to engage in joint action - but actions and objects matter too.
articles.authors: Melanie Y Lam, Lauren Clark, Hope Chambers, Britta Krüger, Johannes Keck
articles.abstract: Emotions are fundamental to social cognition, yet their role in joint action remains underexplored. This study examined how emotional expressions (angry, sad, happy, and neutral) influence behavioural readiness (willingness to engage) in an object-handover task. Across two experiments, participants rated their willingness to "give" or "take" an object (spoon or knife) while viewing dynamic point-light displays (PLDs) of facial (Experiment 1) or bodily (Experiment 2) expressions. Results showed that, although both modalities influenced willingness, guided social decision-making relied on distinct functional heuristics. For facial expressions, a strong emotion-action congruency effect emerged: positive expressions facilitated prosocial "giving", whereas anger, a direct threat cue, inhibited willingness to engage, particularly when paired with a dangerous object. In contrast, bodily expressions were interpreted through a lens of physical vulnerability and predictability. For example, sad and neutral bodies elicited high willingness, suggesting they were perceived as low-threat or stable. Notably, across both experiments, self-directed "take" actions overrode emotional cues, indicating that personal goals may diminish sensitivity to others' expressions. These findings suggest that facial and bodily cues are not functionally equivalent. Rather, they provide complementary social information (intent vs. readiness) that uniquely shapes coordination in joint action contexts.
articles.keywords: Emotional expressions; joint action; object-handover task; point-light displays; willingness to engage
articles.category: Emotion, Facial Expression, Emotion Expression, Social Cognition
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了他人面部和身体情绪表达如何调节人们参与联合行动的意愿。通过动态点光显示呈现愤怒、悲伤、快乐和中性情绪表达，实验发现面部表达通过情绪-动作一致性影响决策（积极表情促进给予，愤怒抑制参与），而身体表达则基于物理脆弱性和可预测性进行解释。此外，个人目标（如获取物体）会覆盖情绪线索，表明自我导向行动降低了对他人情绪的敏感性。

articles.title: Contemptuous expressions during encoding attenuate facial identity recognition in visual working memory in social anxiety: behavioural and electrophysiological evidence.
articles.authors: Mingfan Liu, Guanlai Xiao, Xuzihui Zeng, Linran Li, Li Zhou
articles.abstract: The cognitive-behavioural model emphasises that processing biases are central to the development and maintenance of social anxiety. This study examined how contemptuous facial expressions during encoding influence subsequent recognition of neutral face identity in visual working memory. 32 socially anxious individuals and 32 healthy controls completed a delayed face discrimination task while ERPs were recorded. Behaviourally, socially anxious participants showed lower discrimination sensitivity (d', discrimination power of face identity recognition) and reduced hit rates for faces encoded with contemptuous versus happy expressions, whereas healthy controls showed comparable d' and hit rates across expression conditions. Neurally, socially anxious individuals exhibited larger P1 amplitudes during encoding, enhanced N170 amplitudes for contemptuous faces, and reduced N170 and N250 amplitudes to neutral probes previously encoded with contemptuous expressions. In contrast, healthy controls showed no significant ERP differences between expression conditions. Together, these results indicate that socially threatening cues elicit heightened early perceptual processing but impair the formation and retrieval of facial identity representations, providing converging behavioural and electrophysiological evidence of negative processing bias in social anxiety.
articles.keywords: Contemptuous expressions; Event-related potentials; Facial identity recognition; Social anxiety; Working memory
articles.category: Facial Expression, Face, Emotion, Social Cognition, Emotion Perception
articles.summary: 本研究考察了轻蔑面部表情在编码阶段对社交焦虑个体视觉工作记忆中面部身份识别的影响。行为实验显示，社交焦虑参与者在编码轻蔑表情时面部辨别敏感度降低，电生理数据则揭示了早期感知加工增强但身份表征受损的神经机制，为社交焦虑中的负性加工偏差提供了证据。

articles.title: The unique effect of the number of children on mothers' acceptance of children's negative emotions.
articles.authors: Jackie A Nelson, Carlos Carona, Helena Moreira, Ana Fonseca
articles.abstract: Although parenting research typically focuses on one target child, parents' emotion-related parenting behaviours are predicted by the broader family context. The number of children in the home impacts parents' emotional resources and may be particularly relevant to mothers' beliefs about acceptable emotional displays from their young children. Participants included 226 Portuguese mothers of a 2- to 6-year-old target child (M = 3.89 years) with between 1 and 4 children. Mothers reported on their family makeup, tolerance of their child's dominant and submissive negative emotion displays, parenting stress and mindful parenting. Covarying maternal age, family income and target child age, regressions revealed that mothers with more children were less likely to endorse beliefs that it was acceptable for their young child to display dominant negative emotions. Mothers were also more tolerant of displays of dominant negative emotions for younger children than older children within this early childhood period. These effects remained even after accounting for mothers' parenting stress and mindfulness. Results are interpreted using social information processing, emotion socialisation and transactional stress and coping perspectives.
articles.keywords: Emotion beliefs; early childhood; family size; mothers; parenting stress
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了家庭中孩子数量对母亲接受幼儿负面情绪展示的独特影响。对226名葡萄牙母亲的调查显示，孩子数量更多的母亲对主导负面情绪展示的接受度较低，且对年轻孩子的负面情绪更容忍。这些发现从情绪社会化和社会信息处理视角解释了家庭背景在塑造母亲情绪相关信念中的作用。

articles.title: Attentional bias to situational and implicational information and individual differences in anxiety reactivity and perseveration.
articles.authors: Moore Sophia, Dondzilo Laura, Mazidi Mahdi, Macleod Colin, Notebaert Lies
articles.abstract: There are notable individual differences in how people manage the lead up to, and aftermath of, stressful events. Trait anxiety has consistently been identified as a key factor contributing to these differences. There is growing consensus that trait anxiety is multidimensional, comprising two distinct dimensions, anxiety reactivity and anxiety perseveration. Yet the cognitive mechanisms driving individual differences in these dimensions remain unclear. The present study investigated the potential role of attentional bias towards information differing in content (situational vs. implicational) and valence (negative vs. positive) in driving these individual differences. Undergraduate university students (N = 93) completed a dual-probe assessment task which presented video clips varying in content and valence. State anxiety was assessed at crucial junctures throughout the experimental session to index anxiety reactivity and perseveration. Results revealed attentional bias for negative implicational information played a significant role in predicting anxiety perseveration. Conversely, attention bias for negative situational information was not a significant predictor of anxiety reactivity. Findings are discussed with reference to limitations and future extensions that could build on these findings to address causality and develop interventions capable of modifying cognitive biases.
articles.keywords: Anxiety; attention; cognitive processes; perseveration; reactivity
articles.summary: 本研究探讨了对情境信息和隐含信息的注意力偏差如何影响焦虑的反应性和持续性个体差异。通过双探针视频任务评估大学生的注意力偏差和状态焦虑，结果发现对负面隐含信息的注意力偏差显著预测焦虑持续性，但对负面情境信息的注意力偏差不预测焦虑反应性。

articles.title: Unpacking mentalising through its denial: Integrating the roles of perceptual and contextual cues
articles.authors: Jeroen Vaes, Daniela Ruzzante, Carolina Biasiotti
articles.keywords: Unpacking; Perception; Psychology; Cognitive psychology; Social psychology; Communication; Social perception; Categorical perception; Visual perception
articles.summary: 摘要缺失，无法生成自动总结。

articles.title: Scaffolding the Social Mind: Emotion Recognition Supports Mentalizing but Reinforces Bias.
articles.authors: C Biasiotti, D Ruzzante, F Meconi, J Vaes
articles.abstract: Mentalizing-inferring others' mental states-is a core social function supported by both perceptual and contextual processes. Emotion recognition similarly unfolds in two stages: early perceptual decoding of facial features followed by context-sensitive interpretation. Here, we tested whether emotion recognition scaffolds mentalizing across these stages and whether they attenuate or amplify social bias. Participants (N = 34) completed a face categorization task during EEG recording, in which mentalizing was operationalized as differential neural responses to mindful (human) versus mindless (doll-like) faces, while perceptual (neutral vs. smiling) and contextual (ingroup vs. outgroup) cues were systematically manipulated. Early neural responses differentiated human from doll-like faces, indicating rapid mind detection despite perceptual similarity. Smiling expressions enhanced early neural sensitivity across both human and doll-like faces, suggesting that emotional information is extracted at early perceptual stages independently of stimulus humanity. Later EEG components revealed an asymmetry: emotional expressions selectively boosted mental state attribution for ingroup faces, reflecting the integration of contextual social information at later stages. Implicit association tests confirmed this pattern, with mind-related concepts more strongly associated with smiling ingroup faces.
articles.keywords: emotion recognition; event-related potential; mentalizing; oddball paradigm
articles.category: Emotion, Emotion Perception, Facial Expression, Face, Social Cognition
articles.summary: 本研究利用事件相关电位（ERP）技术，探究情绪识别如何支持心理化过程并影响社会偏见。实验通过操纵面部表情（中性或微笑）和社会分类线索（内群体或外群体），发现微笑表情在早期感知阶段被提取，但在后期神经活动中选择性地增强了对内群体成员的心理状态归因，从而强化了社会偏见。

articles.title: Fear, anxiety, and the extended amygdala-Absence of evidence for strict functional segregation.
articles.authors: Paige R Didier, Shannon E Grogans, Claire M Kaplan, Hyung Cho Kim, Samiha Islam, Allegra S Anderson, Rachael M Tillman, Manuel Kuhn, Juyoen Hur, Andrew S Fox, Kathryn A DeYoung, Jason F Smith, Alexander J Shackman
articles.abstract: Since the time of Freud, the distinction between fear and anxiety has been a hallmark of influential models of emotion and emotional illness, including the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM) and Research Domain Criteria (RDoC). Fear and anxiety disorders are common, debilitating, and challenging to treat, underscoring the importance of developing accurate models of the underlying neurobiology. Although there is consensus that the extended amygdala (EA) plays a central role in orchestrating responses to threat, the respective contributions of its two major subdivisions-the central nucleus of the amygdala (Ce) and bed nucleus of the stria terminalis (BST)-remain contentious. To help adjudicate this debate, we performed a harmonized mega-analysis of fMRI data acquired from 295 adults as they completed a well-established threat-anticipation paradigm. Contrary to popular double-dissociation models, results demonstrated that the Ce responds to temporally uncertain threat and the BST responds to certain threat. In direct comparisons, the two regions showed statistically indistinguishable responses, with strong Bayesian evidence of regional equivalence. In contrast, frontocortical regions responded preferentially to uncertain-threat anticipation. Together, these observations underscore the need to reformulate conceptual models that posit a strict segregation of temporally certain and uncertain threat processing in the EA.
articles.keywords: Research Domain Criteria; affective neuroscience; bed nucleus of the stria terminalis; extended amygdala; fear and anxiety
articles.summary: 本研究通过fMRI元分析探讨扩展杏仁核在恐惧和焦虑处理中的功能，发现中央杏仁核和终纹床核对不确定威胁和确定威胁的反应无显著差异，缺乏严格功能分离的证据。这挑战了传统双分离模型，提示需要重构威胁处理的神经机制概念框架。

articles.title: Differential Responses to Group and Individual Emotional Voices: A Behavioral and ERP Study.
articles.authors: Shuqin Liu, Min Shao, Yudie Zhang, Lu Chen, Lu Yu, Rong Huang, Jiayi Shi, Jingyang Yu, Jing Meng
articles.abstract: Human beings live in social groups, and processing group emotions is crucial for interacting with and understanding others' behaviors and feelings. The present study investigated the cognitive and neural mechanisms of processing group versus individual emotional voices within the auditory modality. We examined ERP responses of 34 participants to group and individual voices across three emotional valences (negative, neutral, positive). Behavioral results revealed a significant interaction: group voices yielded shorter reaction times for neutral and positive emotions but longer for negative ones, alongside more positive overall emotional reactions. Electrophysiologically, group voices elicited more positive N1 and larger P2 amplitudes, with shorter latencies. More specifically, for negative and positive voices, group voices elicited larger P2 amplitudes than individual voices, while no significant difference between group and individual voices emerged for neutral voices. Furthermore, P2 amplitudes elicited by group voices were negatively correlated with participants' trait cognitive empathy. These results suggest that group voices elicit stronger early neural responses driven by an interplay of acoustic and social salience. Importantly, rather than demonstrating a direct empathy-specific neural mechanism, the observed negative correlation suggests that trait empathy may play a role in modulating the early sensory detection and attentional processing of complex, collective emotional signals.
articles.category: Emotion, Vocal Expression, Emotion Perception, Social Cognition
articles.summary: 本研究通过行为和ERP方法，探讨了群体与个体情绪声音的处理机制。行为结果显示，群体声音对中性和积极情绪引发更快的反应，但对消极情绪反应较慢，且整体情感评价更积极。神经电生理发现，群体声音引发更大的早期ERP成分（如N1和P2），表明其具有更强的声学和社交显著性；P2振幅与个体特质认知共情呈负相关，提示共情可能调节对集体情绪信号的早期感知。

articles.title: Human Brain Network for Reading the Mind from the Eyes: Beyond Activated and Deactivated Regions.
articles.authors: Maya Zheltyakova, Maxim Kireev, Artem Myznikov, Ruslan Masharipov, Mikhail Didur, Denis Cherednichenko, Alexander Korotkov
articles.abstract: BOLD signal changes in the Theory of Mind (ToM) network often reflect reduced deactivation during target conditions, yet these below-baseline effects remain largely unaddressed. Using fMRI data from an extended version of the Reading the Mind in the Eyes Test, we examined task-modulated functional connectivity (TMFC) of these areas. We revealed that, despite below-baseline BOLD responses during the reading the mind in the eyes (RME) task, TPJ-pSTS subregions in the left middle and superior temporal gyri, and angular gyrus (AG) demonstrated TMFC patterns closely resembling those of ToM network areas in the pSTS and inferior frontal gyrus (IFG) showing above-baseline activations. The RME task-associated network included regions supporting affective mentalizing (bilateral TPJ-pSTS, IFG, middle frontal gyri, precentral gyri, supplementary motor area) and face-processing areas. These areas also elicited reduced TMFC with ToM network subregions in the AG, supramarginal gyrus, precuneus, and medial prefrontal cortex, associated with cognitive mentalizing. These findings demonstrate that regions with greater BOLD responses during the RME task, despite these responses remaining below baseline, are functionally engaged. Together, the results indicate a dynamic interaction between ToM network subcomponents during the RME task, characterized by engagement of the affective subcomponent and its functional segregation from the cognitive subcomponent.
articles.keywords: BOLD signal; RMET; Reading the Mind in the Eyes Test; Theory of Mind; temporoparietal junction
articles.summary: 本研究通过扩展版的“阅读心灵的眼神测试”结合 fMRI，考察了理论心理网络的功能连接。尽管某些脑区在任务中 BOLD 信号低于基线，但它们仍显示出与情感心智化网络相似的功能连接模式。研究发现，情感心智化子组件与认知心智化子组件在任务中呈现动态交互和功能分离。

articles.title: Age-related neural dynamics of empathy in preschoolers: An EEG study with naturalistic viewing.
articles.authors: Ruoxi Wu, Yiming Zhang, Feiyu Quan, Yan Liang, Han Zhang, Chenbo Wang
articles.abstract: Empathy development in early childhood involves complex neurocognitive processes, but the neural mechanisms underlying this development have yet to be fully explored. This study combined EEG microstate and spectral analyses to investigate empathy-related neural processes in 71 typically developing preschoolers (aged 3-6 years) during naturalistic viewing of socioemotional animations. Spectral analysis revealed increased occipital theta, centrotemporal alpha and temporal beta activities during empathy-eliciting clips compared to baseline. Theta activity in response to Theory of Mind (ToM) clips negatively correlated with children's negative emotion understanding, while alpha activity during Emotional Contagion (EC) clips positively correlated with positive emotion resonance. Age-related decreases in occipital theta power and increases in centrotemporal alpha power were observed, paralleling behavioral improvements in prosocial helping behavior. Microstate analysis identified hierarchical age-related shifts: reduced coverage of microstate MS5 and increased transitions from MS5 to MS2 during ToM processing, implying that as children mature, perspective-taking efficiency increases, redirecting neural resources to response preparation. These findings suggest an age-related neural cascade framework where perceptual efficiency gains may free cognitive resources for mentalizing and emotion regulation, enabling more advanced prosocial actions. The present work suggests that EEG spectral and microstate metrics serve as sensitive neurophysiological indices for assessing early childhood age-related changes in empathy within naturalistic contexts.
articles.keywords: EEG microstates; Empathy; Neural oscillations; child development
articles.summary: 本研究通过EEG微状态和频谱分析，探讨了3-6岁学龄前儿童在观看社会情感动画时的共情相关神经动态。发现儿童特定脑区的θ波、α波和β波活动与情绪理解能力相关，且这些神经活动随年龄增长呈现特定变化模式，支持一个年龄相关的神经级联框架，表明感知效率提升可能释放认知资源用于心理化和情绪调节，从而促进更先进的亲社会行为。

articles.title: A Multimethod Assessment of Spontaneous Behavioral Synchrony in Race- and Age-Concordant Versus Discordant Dyads
articles.authors: Morgan D. Stosic, Adele E. Weaver, Ken Fujiwara, Ishabel M. Vicaria, Derek M. Isaacowitz, Mollie A. Ruben
articles.abstract: This work examines the impact of race and age concordance on behavioral synchrony and self-reported rapport in social interactions, employing a multimethod approach across three studies ( N total = 450) and a mini meta-analysis. Behavioral synchrony was assessed with reliable human coders and OpenPose, and rapport was measured through self-reports. Results indicated that race- and age-concordant dyads exhibited significantly greater behavioral synchrony than discordant dyads, suggesting that shared visible social identities may be associated with smoother, more connected interactions. Synchrony effects were stronger when measured by human coders compared to OpenPose, potentially reflecting human coders' biases related to perceived similarity between interactants or coders' more nuanced ability to detect certain components of synchrony compared to technological approaches. No significant effects of race or age concordance on self-reported rapport were observed. These findings highlight an important factor in predicting the spontaneous emergence of behavioral synchrony and emphasize the value of integrating multimethod approaches.
articles.keywords: age; behavioral synchrony; nonverbal behavior; race; rapport
articles.summary: 本研究采用多方法评估种族和年龄一致性对社会互动中自发行为同步及自报融洽关系的影响。结果发现，种族和年龄一致的配对行为同步显著更高，但融洽关系评分无显著差异；人类编码员比OpenPose技术检测到更强的同步效应，可能源于感知相似性偏差或技术局限。这些发现揭示了社会身份在行为同步中的作用，并强调了整合多方法研究的价值。

articles.title: Just Like My CEO: When Perceived Similarity Makes Pay Inequality Acceptable
articles.authors: Daniel Heller, Garrett L. Brady, M. Ena Inesi, Thomas Mussweiler
articles.abstract: Large CEO-employee pay gaps often elicit negative reactions, yet they have reached historic highs with limited resistance. How can this be explained? Developing a novel social comparison perspective, we propose that under specific circumstances pay inequality may yield positive reactions. We argue that perceived similarity with a CEO enhances acceptance of CEO pay, a process driven by elevated inspiration. We tested this prediction in four studies: a large international archival survey, a pre-registered survey of U.S. employees, and two pre-registered experiments. In the archival survey, we demonstrate that perceived similarity to CEOs, when measured indirectly, reduced support for income-inequality-reduction policies, and this effect appears-to-emerge across cultures. Converging experimental evidence reveals that employees who perceived greater similarity to their CEOs are more accepting of high CEO pay, due to feeling more inspired. These findings help elucidate why extreme pay disparities are often accepted, even though they may reinforce broader societal inequality.
articles.keywords: CEO; inspiration; leadership; local pay inequality; social comparison
articles.summary: 本研究提出了一个社会比较新视角，探讨感知相似性如何通过激发灵感来提高员工对CEO高薪的接受度。通过国际档案调查、员工调查和实验研究，发现员工感知到与CEO相似性越高，越能接受高薪差距，这一效应在跨文化背景下普遍存在，主要源于灵感的提升。这些发现有助于解释极端薪酬不平等为何常被接受，尽管可能加剧社会不平等。

articles.title: Fluency as a Cue to Authenticity
articles.authors: Lydia Needy, Matthew Baldwin, Rebecca J. Schlegel, Wilhelm Hofmann
articles.abstract: The quest for authenticity is a potent existential striving. Commonly defined as knowing and living in accordance with one's perceived "true self," we propose that authenticity may also be inferred from ambient feelings of fluency, or the subjective feeling of ease that corresponds to one's immediate experience, mental processing, or physical action. We report four studies ( N = 1,465) supporting this idea. Study 1 shows that fluency during a recent activity predicts subjective authenticity beyond other relevant variables. Study 2 demonstrates that participants' recalled fluent experiences also felt authentic. In Study 3, generating self-defining attributes under cognitive load reduced fluency and, subsequently, subjective authenticity. Finally, preregistered Study 4 manipulated fluency during a non-self-relevant task. Participants in the fluent condition reported greater subjective authenticity than those in the disfluent condition. We discuss how a phenomenological approach to subjective authenticity can integrate and complement recent theorizing about the nature of authenticity.
articles.keywords: authenticity; fluency; phenomenology; self-concept; true self
articles.summary: 本研究提出流畅感——即个体在即时体验、心理处理或行动时的主观轻松感——可以作为真实性的线索。通过四项实验，研究发现流畅感能显著预测主观真实性，而认知负荷等操作通过影响流畅感间接改变真实性判断。

articles.title: Solidarity With Friends, Relationship Status, and Mental Health During the COVID-19 Pandemic
articles.journal: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships
articles.publish_info: Journal of Social and Personal Relationships, 2026-06-05
articles.abstract: While the solidarity paradigm has been extensively applied to intergenerational relationships, its relevance to adult friendships remains largely unexplored. Therefore, we aimed to uncover distinct types of solidarity with friends among adults and examine whether these types are associated with mental health (depressive symptoms, psychological well-being, and self-esteem) during the COVID-19 pandemic. Furthermore, we examined the moderating role of relationship status (partnered vs. unpartnered) on the above associations. We analyzed 1,353 adults (age range: 17-91) from the Longitudinal Study of Generations (2022) Wave. Latent class analysis with three-step approach was conducted. Four latent classes of solidarity with friends were identified: (1) Tight-knit with many friends , (2) Social with many friends , (3) Functional with few friends , and (4) Detached with few friends . Furthermore, individuals who were identified in the Tight-knit with many friends and Social with many friends latent classes reported better mental health than those in the Detached with few friends latent class. Regarding the moderation effects, we found that unpartnered individuals in the Detached with few friends latent class reported poorer mental health than partnered counterparts, compared to those in Tight-knit with many friends and Social with many friends latent classes. Our findings indicate that solidarity with friends plays a crucial role in promoting mental health in adulthood, especially for those who are unpartnered. By extending the solidarity paradigm to the realm of adult friendships, this study lays a foundation for further inquiry into social networks within family science.
articles.keywords: Solidarity; Mental health; Latent class model; Psychology; Realm; Moderation; Friendship; Social psychology; Relevance; Pandemic
articles.summary: 本研究通过潜在类别分析，识别出成人友谊中四种团结类型，并考察这些类型与COVID-19大流行期间心理健康的关系。研究发现，紧密型和社交型团结与更好的心理健康相关，而非伴侣个体在分离型团结中报告更差心理健康，表明朋友团结对心理健康有重要影响。
